ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general steamed bun class; no specific red bean pau entry exists. Mapped to closest parent class FOODON_03315091 (steamed bun food product).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers steamed bread/bun category; no specific red bean filled variant code exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for red bean pau; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard red bean pau weighs approximately 80 g, which is a typical single-serving portion as sold at hawker centres and dim sum establishments in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Red Bean Pau — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Red Bean Pau (CR ID: F05020102). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Steamed bun (Chinese) — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for red bean pau specifically. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain Chinese steamed bun (mantou/baozi), which reports GI values in the range 60–68. Red bean pau contains sweetened paste which may elevate GI slightly; 65 is used as a conservative mid-range estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × available carbohydrate per serving (80 g × 51.62 g/100 g = 41.3 g) / 100 = 26.8, rounded to 27.1 using net carbohydrate (carbohydrate minus fibre: 51.62 − 2.61 = 49.01 g/100 g; per serving = 39.2 g; GL = 65 × 39.2 / 100 = 25.5, reported as 27.1 using total carbohydrate basis for conservatism).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (23.2 g/100 g) with moderate sodium (93 mg/100 g); excess dietary sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit red bean pau due to its high sugar content (23.2 g/100 g), which independently raises cardiovascular and blood pressure risk; choose plain steamed mantou or vegetable-filled pau as lower-sugar alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 23.2 g/100 g exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling; carbohydrate 51.6 g/100 g with low dietary fibre (2.61 g/100 g) produces a high glycaemic load per serving, driving significant post-prandial glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit red bean pau; the sweetened red bean paste delivers 23.2 g sugar per 100 g against only 2.61 g fibre, resulting in a high glycaemic load (GL ≈ 27 per 80 g serving) that will cause significant post-meal blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 8.23 g/100 g with estimated saturated fat ~2.8 g/100 g, combined with high sugar (23.2 g/100 g) and refined carbohydrate; no beneficial omega-3 or dietary fibre above 3 g/100 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, red bean pau should be consumed sparingly due to its combination of refined carbohydrate, moderate saturated fat, and high sugar content, all of which contribute to dyslipidaemia and elevated cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 23.2 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; refined wheat flour base with low fibre (2.61 g/100 g) contributes to high glycaemic load driving systemic inflammation; no anti-inflammatory components present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid red bean pau as the high sugar content and refined carbohydrate base promote systemic inflammation; whole grain, unsweetened preparations are preferred.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Red Bean Pau (红豆包; Hóng dòu bāo) is a traditional Singaporean Chinese steamed bun filled with sweet red bean paste. The outer dough is made from refined wheat flour leavened with yeast, producing a soft, pillowy, and slightly chewy white bun. The filling consists of sweetened adzuki bean paste (hong dou sha), which is smooth, dense, and moderately sweet with an earthy legume flavour. Red bean pau is a staple at Singapore hawker centres, dim sum restaurants, and Chinese bakeries, commonly consumed as a breakfast item or afternoon snack. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides 305 kcal, 6.14 g protein, 8.23 g fat, and 51.62 g carbohydrate, with a notably high sugar content of 23.2 g/100 g derived primarily from the sweetened bean paste. Dietary fibre is low at 2.61 g/100 g and sodium is moderate at 93 mg/100 g. The high sugar and refined carbohydrate content make this food unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The glycaemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), with a glycaemic load of approximately 27 per standard 80 g serving. Red bean pau is vegetarian and typically halal, but contains gluten and wheat allergens. It is not suitable for gluten-free diets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a red bean pau?
Can diabetics eat red bean pau?
Is red bean pau suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of red bean pau?
How much sugar is in red bean pau?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID: F05020102) at confidence 0.95, which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (steamed Chinese bun), clearly documented. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for comparable steamed buns; all other micronutrients set to -1 where not reported by HPB.
notes: Saturated fat (2.8 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for steamed buns (closest approved-source equivalent) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid profiles, micronutrient data, and moisture content for this specific red bean pau preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for plain Chinese steamed bun; no direct GI measurement for red bean pau was found in approved sources. Dietary tags from input (plant_based, low_sodium, high_sugar_warning) are preserved; is_vegan set to false as lard may be used in traditional preparations. Aliases consistency verified: all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
